title: Set Matrix Zeroes
description: Given a m x n matrix matrix, if an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0. You must do it in place.

examples.explanation: The element at (1,1) is 0, so we set the entire row (1) and column (1) to 0. The resulting matrix has rows and columns updated accordingly.
The first element is 0, which sets the first row and first column to 0. Then, the 0 at (0,3) sets its row and column to 0 as well.
The single element is 0, which means the only row and column are set to 0.

constraints: The input matrix is of size m x n where 1 <= m, n <= 200.
All elements of the matrix are integers.
hints: Consider using the first row and first column to track which rows and columns need to be zeroed.
You may want to traverse the matrix twice to avoid overwriting your zero indicators.
Be careful with the edge cases when the first row or first column contains zeros.
Think about whether you can use a single pass to achieve your result.
